name: Poule-au-Pot (Chicken Pot Pie)
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the chicken into individual pieces and brown lightly in the butter.
2. Add the boiling water, carrot, onion, celery leaves, cloves, garlic, bay leaf, salt, and pepper. Bring to a boil, cover, then simmer over low heat until the chicken is tender.
3. Place the cooked pieces of chicken in a casserole and top with the potatoes, sliced paper-thin.
4. Make a gravy using 3 cups of the consomme left after the chicken has been cooked, 3 tablespoons of the fat from the top of the consomme, and 4 tablespoons of flour. Salt and pepper to taste and pour over the potatoes and chicken.
5. Top the chicken, potatoes, and gravy with a parsley pinwheel, which is prepared as follows:
- Stir together the flour, salt, and baking powder. Cut in the shortening with 2 knives until the particles are the size of peas.
- Add the milk gradually. Toss lightly with a fork until all the flour has been dampened, then knead quickly into a round ball.
- Roll on a floured board into an oblong shape that is 1/8 inch thick.
6. Butter the top of the dough and sprinkle with the minced parsley. Roll the same way you would a jelly roll.
7. Cut in 1 1/2 to 2 inches lengths. Place cut side on top of casserole in an attractive design.
8. Bake at 425 degrees F for 30 to 40 minutes, or until the top is golden brown.
